text:
Once upon a time beside the Black Forest lived Luna. Luna had no tail. She had no wings. She had no magic.

But Luna had feathers. Everywhere.

Every morning she liked to sneak out and watch the animals. Especially rabbits. They were playful. She'd hop there to watch. They'd hop around. Sometimes they'd chase butterflies.

One day while Luna watched a squirrel hide nuts in a bush—a nice snack—it heard something. Silent but deadly.

A hare. Quiet as a mouse.

The hare trotted toward her. Luna's feathers fluttered. What could she do?

Slow step-by-step retreat might work. Luna fluttered softly to another branch. She danced amongst leaves. The bunny didn't notice.

Then it took off.

Luna dashed back to tree. She flew up slowly, checking the bush.

No nuts.

Maybe somewhere else.

She soared. She hovered. Thought perhaps some bird dropped one.

She hovered again. Nothing. She'd rest here.

Minutes tick
